--- source_id: news_manual:african_union_gerd_2020 source_type: official_primary title: African Union communique on GERD negotiations, July 2020 publisher: African Union url: https://au.int/en/pressreleases/20200724/hosg-communique-2nd-meetinng-grand-ethiopian-renaissance-dam-gerd retrieved_at: 2026-06-07 --- The African Union stated that its Bureau of the Assembly of Heads of State and Government met on 21 July 2020 to review progress in trilateral negotiations on the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam involving Egypt, Ethiopia, and Sudan. The communique describes the issues under negotiation as the first filling and annual operation of the GERD and future development projects on the Blue Nile River upstream of the GERD. The AU Bureau said the parties should work toward a binding agreement on filling and operation of the GERD and a broader comprehensive agreement on future Blue Nile developments. This source supports relationship modules about African Union diplomacy, GERD dispute history, deescalation pathways, and unresolved legal/technical issues. It does not by itself establish military intentions, public opinion, or secret red lines.
